Mary Honeyball argues that the significance of the new premiership lies in the possibility of restoring public confidence, rebalancing the relationship between central and local government, and offering a more hopeful national narrative after the political and economic dislocation associated with Brexit.

items.title: Expanded 2026 North American World Cup: Spain's 'diligent' performance to Messi's enduring legacy
items.description: Eve Irvine is pleased to welcome Robbie Knock, Lead Commentator and sports journalist. The 2026 FIFA World Cup will be remembered not only for Spain's triumph on the pitch but also for the way football became enmeshed in politics, entertainment, institutional scrutiny, and the closing chapter of one of the sport's greatest careers. Robbie Knock argues that Spain's victory was the product of tactical superiority rather than fortune, while acknowledging that controversial refereeing decisions complicated perceptions of the tournament. He also presents Lionel Messi's expected international farewell as the conclusion of a defining era in world football, emphasising that dignity in defeat may ultimately reinforce rather than diminish his legacy.

items.title: Who is Khalil al-Hayya, Hamas’s new leader?
items.description: Hamas on Monday named Khalil al-Hayya, one of the founding members of the Palestinian militant group, as its new leader. Hayya is seen as a respected and pragmatic Hamas veteran “who doesn’t get carried away easily”. Last year, he survived an Israeli attack in Doha targeting the Hamas leadership which left several people dead, including one of his sons.
